GenoSim 1.0.0

    • Introduction to GenoSim: Forward-Time Genotype Simulation

Read and Validate a Pedigree CSV File

Source: R/pedigree.R
read_pedigree.Rd

Reads a pedigree file in CSV format, normalises column names, validates the parent-offspring structure, infers generation depths via topological sort, and computes per-individual Wright inbreeding coefficients using a recursive kinship table.

read_pedigree(ped_path, verbose = TRUE)

Arguments

ped_path

Character. Path to the pedigree CSV file. The file must contain at minimum three columns identifying each individual and its parents (see Details for accepted column names). Missing parents should be coded as "0".

verbose

Logical. Print a summary after loading. Default TRUE.

Value

A data.frame with one row per individual and the following columns (additional columns from the input file are preserved):

individual_id

Character. Unique individual identifier.

father_id

Character. Father's ID or "0".

mother_id

Character. Mother's ID or "0".

sex

Integer. 1 = male, 2 = female, 0 = unknown.

phenotype

Integer. 1 = unaffected, 2 = affected, -9 = missing.

family_id

Character. Family identifier.

generation

Integer. Generation depth (founders = 0), inferred topologically if not present in the input file.

is_founder

Logical. TRUE if both parents are absent.

inbreeding_F

Numeric. Wright's inbreeding coefficient \(F_i = \phi(father_i, mother_i)\), computed by recursive kinship.

Details

Accepted column name aliases (case-insensitive):

CanonicalAccepted alternatives
individual_idiid, id, sample_id, ind_id
father_idfid, father, pat, paternal_id
mother_idmid, mother, mat, maternal_id
sexgender
phenotypepheno, affection, status
family_idfamid, fam_id, family
generationgen, generation_num

Missing parent coding: "0", "NA", "", or "." are all treated as absent parents.

Inbreeding coefficient: Computed via the tabular kinship method. \(F_i = \phi(father_i, mother_i)\) where \(\phi\) is the kinship (coefficient of coancestry) computed recursively from founders upward. Founders have \(F = 0\) and \(\phi(i,i) = 0.5\).

See also

summarise_pedigree, read_vcf_cohort, simulate_from_pedigree, plot_pedigree_tree

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
ped <- read_pedigree("family_pedigree.csv")
# Show structure
summarise_pedigree(ped)
# Affected individuals with high inbreeding
ped[ped$phenotype == 2 & ped$inbreeding_F > 0.1, ]
} # }
